title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares fresh dockerfile frontend, build context, instructions, and process contracts decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives · changed case · Quiz Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

Which action demonstrates Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives · changed case · Quiz Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use a pinned stable dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries. For Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives · changed case · Quiz Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives, keep Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.
